events.label: reasoning
events.text: The scenario: trench protection, sloping wrong for soil type, actively in progress. OSHA 1926 Subpart P. Need to identify hazards, severity, immediate controls. Sloping wrong for soil type means the trench slope doesn't match tabulated data for the soil classification. This is a cave-in hazard. Critical risk. Decision fail. Component conditions: non-compliant, installed-defective. Work stage in-progress. Percent complete maybe 50? Value 0. Confidence high. Findings: trench sloped incorrectly for soil type per OSHA tabulated data; cave-in hazard. Actions: stop work, evacuate trench, reclassify soil, correct slope per tabulated data or use protective system (trench box/shoring), competent person inspection. References: OSHA 1926.652, Appendix B.

    "Trench slope does not match OSHA tabulated data for the in-situ soil type, creating a cave-in hazard.",
    "Protective system (sloping/benching) is non-compliant with OSHA 1926 Subpart P requirements.",
    "Workers are exposed in an unprotected or inadequately protected excavation while work is actively in progress."

    "Stop work immediately and evacuate all personnel from the trench.",
    "Have the competent person reclassify the soil and verify the required slope/bench angle per OSHA tabulated data.",

    "Do not allow re-entry until the competent person has inspected and approved the protective system.",
    "Document the non-compliance and notify the third-party inspector/owner's representative."
  ],
  "rationale": "Sloping that is too steep for the actual soil classification violates OSHA 1926 Subpart P and creates an imminent cave-in risk to workers in the excavation. This is a critical, stop-work condition requiring immediate correction before any further work in the trench.",

    "OSHA 1926 Subpart P Appendix B - Sloping and benching tabulated data",
    "OSHA 1926.651(k) - Inspections by a competent person"
  ]
